title: Small Parts Organizer
summary: I wanted to take the time to come up with a small parts organizing solution.
description: I wanted to take the time to come up with a small parts organizing solution. After receiving the Husky interlocking small parts organizers in a recent My Prospective Tool Review shipment, I knew I wanted to build a unit to house them while in the shop. I also wanted this unit to include some easily accessible storage bins for things that I use on a more regular basis like safety glasses, tape measures, pencils, etc

steps.title: Breaking Down Plywood
steps.body: Break down the plywood using the cut list provided below.

steps.title: Cutting the Dados
steps.body: After breaking down the plywood per the cut list, start by layout out all the 3/4″ thick parts as shown. Then, swap out the table saw blade for a blade(s) with a similar thickness to the 1/4″ plywood dividers. Then cut the dados per the diagram below.

steps.title: Install Top and Shelves
steps.body: Per the diagram below, drill the pocket holes and attach the top and shelves to the side, as shown, with 1-1/4″ pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach Other Side
steps.body: Per the diagram below, use the pocket holes drilled in the last step to attach the other side with 1-1/4″ pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Install Back and Bottom
steps.body: Slide the back into place and test the fit of the bottom. If the back is too tight of a fit or the bottom is protruding then you may have to trim a little off the back. Once you have a a good fit, drill pocket holes in the bottom and attach with 1-1/4" pocket screws. Then run a bead of wood glue around the back panel and let dry overnight.

steps.title: Inserting the Dividers
steps.body: Then slide the dividers into place and trim to fit as required. The unit can be set on the ground, casters attached for portability, or hung on the wall with a french cleat.
